description_long: This church features a tall, slender spire rising from a steeply pitched roof, flanked by two smaller pinnacles. The main facade is adorned with a large circular window, likely a rose window, and pointed arch details that evoke a Gothic architectural style. The exterior walls are a mix of brown and beige tones, with the roof and spire rendered in a dark, almost black color. The building has a rectangular base with a prominent gabled section and a flat-roofed annex to one side, possibly for additional rooms or a vestry. Decorative elements include small circular and pointed windows, and the overall structure is solid and imposing, typical of urban churches. The church appears to be constructed primarily from stone or brick, with metal or slate roofing.
description: A Gothic-style church with a tall black spire, brown and beige walls, and a large circular window on the front facade. The structure is solid, with pointed arches and a flat-roofed annex.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steeply pitched roof, the tall central spire, and the flat-roofed annex with a railing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large circular window, pointed arches, and the symmetrical arrangement of the spire and pinnacles.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the spire and pinnacles visible, but less detail on the facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gabled roof and annex, with the spire and pinnacles in profile.
